title: Seqlok Error Registry
description: JSON Schema describing the export format of the Seqlok error registry (domains, codes, numeric encodings, and metadata).

schemaVersion.description: Schema version for this export format. Increment on breaking changes.

generatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the registry was generated.

generator.description: Identifier of the tool that generated this registry export.

domainIds.description: Mapping from domain prefix (e.g., 'backing', 'spec') to numeric domain ID (0–255).

domains.description: All error domains registered in the system.

allCodes.description: Flattened list of all error codes across all domains. Convenience view for tools.

RegistryStats.description: Aggregate statistics about the error registry.

totalCodes.description: Total number of error codes across all domains.

severityCounts.description: Error count grouped by severity level.

Domain.description: A single error domain with its codes.

prefix.description: Domain prefix used in fully-qualified error codes (e.g., 'backing' in 'backing.allocFailed').

domainId.description: Numeric domain ID used in the error code encoding scheme (high byte of the 32-bit numeric code).

codes.description: All error codes belonging to this domain.

ErrorCode.description: A single error code with its metadata.

code.description: Fully-qualified error code in the format 'domain.key' (e.g., 'backing.allocFailed').

numericCode.description: 32-bit numeric error code encoding (domainId << 24) | localId. Used by native integrations and binary protocols.

domain.description: Domain prefix extracted from the code (e.g. 'backing').

ErrorMeta.description: Metadata describing an error code's semantics and handling hints.

severity.description:
Coarse severity classification:
- 'warning': recoverable, usually non-fatal
- 'error': operation failed, but process may continue
- 'fatal': unrecoverable, process or worker should be torn down

recoverable.description: Whether recovery is plausible in principle (e.g. transient vs structural failure).

boundarySafe.description: Whether it is safe to surface this error across trust boundaries (true = OK for UIs / APIs, false = internal-only).

docsUrl.description: Optional documentation URL for 'learn more' links.

tags.description: Optional tags used by tooling / dashboards.

domainHint.description: Optional hint about the dominant resource or subsystem (e.g. 'memory', 'env', 'binding').
